markdown.markdown:
```{=html}
Track 01 · Systems Programming

C: Closest
to the machine.

The language that built Unix, Linux, and almost everything underneath modern software. Learn C and you'll understand how computers actually work — memory, pointers, and all.

⏱ Duration: 6 months 👥 Level: Beginner → Advance 📦 Projects 4 Builds 🔑 Includes Git & GitHub
Curriculum

6 Months, Zero Hand-Holding

C doesn't hide things from you — and neither does this course. You'll leave knowing exactly what happens when your code runs.

01
C Fundamentals
Variables, data types, operators, control flow, and the compilation pipeline from .c to executable.
gcc / clangint/float/charif/for/whilescanf/printf
02
Functions & Scope
Prototypes, pass-by-value, recursion, and understanding the call stack frame by frame.
PrototypesRecursionStack framesReturn values
03
Arrays & Strings
Arrays as contiguous memory, pointer-array duality, C-strings, and standard string library functions.
ArraysC-stringsstring.hPointer arithmetic
04
Pointers & Memory
The heart of C. Pointer types, dereferencing, pointer-to-pointer, and visualising memory layouts.
* and &Pointer typesvoid*NULL
05
Dynamic Memory
Heap allocation with malloc/calloc/realloc, freeing memory properly, and detecting memory leaks with Valgrind.
mallocfreeValgrindMemory leaks
06
Structs & Unions
Custom data types, struct padding, typedef, and building simple linked data structures from scratch.
structuniontypedefPadding
07
File I/O
fopen, fread, fwrite, fprintf — reading and writing binary and text files, and error handling with errno.
fopen/fcloseBinary fileserrnofseek
08
Data Structures in C
Implement linked lists, stacks, queues, and a basic hash table using only C — no libraries, no shortcuts.
Linked ListStackQueueHash Table
Memory Model

You'll actually understand this.

Most courses skip over memory. This one makes it the centrepiece — because everything else flows from it.

Stack — grows downward
0x7fff...f0
main()
← current frame
0x7fff...d0
int x = 42
local variable
0x7fff...c8
int *p
← pointer on stack
Heap — grows upward
0x55...1000
malloc(100)
← *p points here
0x55...1064
[free block]
must call free()
Projects

Four Things You'll Build in C

No toy programs. Each project solves a real problem and teaches you something you can't learn from reading.

Project 01
Custom Shell (mini-bash)
Build a minimal Unix shell that reads commands, forks processes, handles pipes and redirects. Real systems programming.
fork/execpipessignals
Project 02
Memory Allocator
Implement your own malloc() and free() using sbrk. Handle fragmentation, coalescing, and alignment correctly.
sbrkHeapFragmentation
Project 03
Linked List Library
A complete doubly-linked list with insert, delete, search, and iterator — with tests for every function.
StructsPointersTesting
Project 04
File Compression Tool
Implement run-length encoding to compress and decompress binary files. Process I/O and bitwise operations.
File I/OBitwise opsRLE
Admission Open • Limited Seats

Understand code at the
deepest level.

C makes every other language feel transparent. Once you know C, you know how computing actually works.

Enroll Now
Track 02 · Object-Oriented Programming

C++: Power
 meets elegance.

From C foundations to full OOP mastery. Classes, templates, the STL, and modern C++17/20 features — the language of competitive programming, game engines, and systems that demand performance.

⏱ Duration: 6 Months 👥 Level: Beginner → Advance 📦 Projects 4 Builds 🔑 Includes Git & GitHub
Curriculum

10 Weeks of Modern C++

We build on C knowledge and push into OOP, templates, and the full STL — ending with performance patterns used in real production code.

01
C → C++ Transition
References, inline functions, default arguments, namespaces, and cin/cout. C experience required.
References &Namespacescout/cinauto keyword
02
Classes & Objects
Constructors, destructors, member functions, access modifiers, and the this pointer.
classConstructor~Destructorthis ptr
03
Operator Overloading
Overload +, ==, <<, [], and more. Write classes that feel natural and intuitive to use.
operator+operator==friendoperator[]
04
Inheritance & Polymorphism
Base and derived classes, virtual functions, pure virtual, abstract classes, and vtable internals.
virtualoverrideabstractvtable
05
Templates
Function templates, class templates, template specialisation, and how the STL is built on top of them.
template<T>SpecialisationVariadic templates
06
STL Deep Dive
vector, list, deque, map, unordered_map, set, priority_queue — when to use which and why.
vectormap/setpriority_queueIterators
07
Smart Pointers & RAII
unique_ptr, shared_ptr, weak_ptr. Resource Acquisition Is Initialization — the pattern that makes C++ safe.
unique_ptrshared_ptrRAIImove semantics
08
Modern C++17/20
Lambdas, ranges, structured bindings, std::optional, std::variant, and concepts.
Lambdasrangesoptionalconcepts
Projects

Four Builds That Show What C++ Can Do

These aren't just exercises — they're demonstrations of what C++ enables that other languages struggle to match.

Project 01
Custom Vector<T> Template
Implement your own resizable array from scratch — push_back, pop_back, iterators, copy semantics, and move semantics.
TemplatesIteratorsMove semantics
Project 02
Text-Based RPG with OOP
Full class hierarchy — Character, Enemy, Weapon, Item — using inheritance, virtual functions, and polymorphism throughout.
OOPPolymorphismSTL
Project 03
Expression Parser
Parse and evaluate math expressions like "(3 + 4) * 2" — using a recursive descent parser, stack, and operator precedence.
ParsingRecursionStack
Project 04
Thread-Safe Queue
Build a concurrent message queue using std::mutex, std::condition_variable, and smart pointers. Introduction to concurrency.
ThreadsMutexConcurrency
Admission Open • Limited Seats

The language of
serious software.

Competitive programmers, game developers, and systems engineers all use C++. Join a track that teaches it properly.

Enroll Now
Track 03 · Python

Python: Scripting
to AI/ML

The most versatile language in tech. Start with clean fundamentals, build automation tools, work with real datasets, and write your first machine learning models — all in one structured track.

⏱ Duration: 6 Months 👥 Level: Beginner → Advanced 📦 Projects 5 Real Projects 🔑 Includes Git & GitHub
Curriculum

10 Weeks of Real Python

Every week builds directly on the last — from variables to deployed ML models. No random topic jumps.

01
Python Foundations
Variables, data types, control flow, functions, and comprehensions. Write clean, idiomatic Python from day one.
VariablesLoopsFunctionsList Comprehensions
02
OOP & Modules
Classes, inheritance, dunder methods, and structuring code into reusable modules and packages.
ClassesInheritance__init__Packages
03
File I/O & Automation
Read, write, parse files (CSV, JSON, XML). Automate repetitive tasks — rename files, generate reports, scrape data.
open()CSV/JSONos moduleAutomation
04
NumPy & Pandas
The backbone of data work. Array operations, DataFrames, cleaning messy data, groupby, merging, and aggregation.
NumPy arraysDataFramesgroupbyData Cleaning
05
Data Visualisation
Turn raw data into insight. Matplotlib for control, Seaborn for aesthetics, Plotly for interactive charts.
MatplotlibSeabornPlotlyDashboard basics
06
APIs & Web Scraping
Fetch live data from REST APIs with requests, scrape websites with BeautifulSoup, and handle rate limits gracefully.
requestsREST APIsBeautifulSoupJSON parsing
07
ML Foundations
Regression, classification, train/test splits, overfitting, evaluation metrics. Build intuition before touching a neural network.
scikit-learnLinear RegressionClassificationCross-validation
08
ML in Production
Save models with joblib, build a simple prediction API with FastAPI, and deploy it on a cloud VM.
joblibFastAPIModel DeploymentDocker basics
Projects

Five Things You'll Build

Real projects, real outcomes. Everything goes to GitHub and counts as portfolio work.

Project 01
File Organiser CLI Tool
Automate sorting a messy downloads folder — rename, categorise, and log every action. Your first useful script.
ospathlibargparse
Project 02
News API Dashboard
Pull headlines from a live API, filter by topic, and render a clean terminal dashboard with rich.
requestsAPIrich
Project 03
EDA Report Generator
Upload any CSV, get back a full exploratory data analysis report with statistics and charts — automated with Pandas & Matplotlib.
PandasMatplotlibSeaborn
Project 04
House Price ML Model
Train a regression model on real estate data, tune hyperparameters, evaluate rigorously, and produce a prediction report.
scikit-learnRegressionCross-val
Project 05
Prediction API (FastAPI)
Wrap your ML model in a FastAPI endpoint, write tests with pytest, containerise with Docker, and deploy on AWS EC2.
FastAPIDockerAWS EC2
Admission Open • Limited Seats

Python opens
every door.

Web, data, ML, automation — it's all Python. Start here, and every other course becomes easier.

Enroll Now
```
